title: Review: Zinc Language Spec
notes:
DX rating: AWKWARD — ambient allocators and contracts are well-motivated but the using/with two-keyword design, the invalid layout syntax examples, and the hidden debug-mode allocations will cause repeated friction for Zig callers who expect explicitness throughout
Confidence: 75% chance of missed design flaw. Least confident flags: (1) 8rf.7 (using as function coloring) — the with block scope limit may make this substantially better than async coloring; I haven't seen large codebases using this pattern. (2) 8rf.16 (STM copy-only limitation) — possibly intentional given the performance section explicitly recommends atomics for scalars. Would update assessment if: shown a real call site using function pointers to using(allocator) functions, or shown how the Zig compiler actually handles the std.meta.Integer parameter type syntax.
